title: Learn about your taxes - Educator toolkit - Canada.ca
content:
Learn about your taxes
Empower your students with essential tax knowledge with the Canada Revenue Agency's (CRA) Learn about your taxes toolkit. Designed to support educators in teaching tax literacy effectively, this toolkit offers a range of resources to engage and educate students about their tax responsibilities and financial literacy. Be sure to check back regularly and follow CRA on social media for any updates!
We have included a variety of resources below that can be used to spread the word to your classroom or organization members.
- Ways to get involved
- Learn about your taxes resources
- Additional resources
Print out the following posters, worksheets and infographics to supplement your lesson plans and reinforce key tax literacy concepts in class. They are great for visual learners and make learning about taxes more interactive and fun!
Print this colourable poster (PDF, 161 Kb) for your classroom.
- Crossword puzzle (PDF, 254 Kb)
- Yes or no questions (PDF, 70 Kb)
- Word search (PDF, 213 Kb)
- Term matchup (PDF, 74 Kb)
- How to fix a mistake on your tax return

## Ways to get involved
For the latest Learn about your taxes news, follow us on our social media channels and search for the hashtag #HowTaxesWork.
- Follow the CRA on Facebook
- Follow the CRA on Linkedin
- Follow the CRA on Instagram
- Follow the CRA on Youtube
- Listen to our Taxology podcast
Copy these posts and use them on your social media channels to share the toolkit.

There are ready to use modules and resources that can help you easily incorporate taxes into your curriculum.
Get the tools you need: New! Educator toolkit
Download these visuals and use them on your website or social media channels.
On a PC: Right-click on the image you want to download. On a Mac: Hold down the Control key (Ctrl) and click on the image you want to download. On a mobile device: Tap and hold the image you want to download until the menu appears.

#### (2) Choose “Save as”
The options on the contextual menu will vary, depending on which web browser you’re using. Some browsers may say “Save as” while others may say “Save image as” or “Save picture as.”

#### (3) Rename the file (optional)
A dialogue box will open, giving you the opportunity to change the file name or keep it as is.

#### (4) Choose a destination folder
Specify a location to save the downloaded image and click “Save.”
An individual with long hair, wearing a plaid blazer, stands against an orange background with a dark green horizontal stripe at the bottom. The person is holding a stack of bright pink, yellow and green notebooks. To the right side of the image, there are three white hexagonal outlines stacked vertically.
Use this banner for your online content.

## Learn about your taxes resources
- Learn about your taxes Check out the Canada Revenue Agency's online learning tool to learn how to file your taxes, what benefits and credits may be available to you, and so much more. It'll also explain how the tax system works and why taxes matter.
Learn about your taxes
Check out the Canada Revenue Agency's online learning tool to learn how to file your taxes, what benefits and credits may be available to you, and so much more. It'll also explain how the tax system works and why taxes matter.
- The one about the TD1 form, part 1 Learn how to fill out a Personal Tax Credits Return or TD1 form correctly, when starting a job.
The one about the TD1 form, part 1
Learn how to fill out a Personal Tax Credits Return or TD1 form correctly, when starting a job.
- The one about your paystub Learn what's in a typical pay stub, why is money being taken off every time, and where that money goes.
The one about your paystub
Learn what's in a typical pay stub, why is money being taken off every time, and where that money goes.
- The one about doing your taxes Learn the basic steps to filling out a tax return online. Watch part 1 first to see what documents you need before getting started.
The one about doing your taxes
Learn the basic steps to filling out a tax return online. Watch part 1 first to see what documents you need before getting started.
